task_context.actionable_question: Which demographic groups have the highest failure or dropout rate?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: For each demographic group, state fail rate and withdrawal rate as percentages. Flag groups where fail+withdrawn > cohort threshold. Note: output is categorical final_outcome — distinct from A-G08 which uses continuous avg_score.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: The analysis of the pass, fail, and withdrawal rates across different demographic groups reveals significant disparities, particularly in the 0-10% and 10-20% groups, where the combined fail and withdrawal rates exceed the cohort threshold. The 0-10% group exhibits the highest withdrawal rate, indicating a critical area for intervention.

Insights: High Withdrawal Rate in 0-10% Group: The 0-10% group has a withdrawal rate of 53.8%, which is the highest among all groups analyzed, indicating a significant challenge in student retention. | Combined Fail and Withdrawal Rates Exceeding Threshold: In the 0-10% group, the combined fail (13.8%) and withdrawal (53.8%) rates total 67.6%, significantly exceeding the cohort threshold, indicating a critical need for targeted support. | Consistent Withdrawal Rates Across Groups: The withdrawal rates across the 10-20% and 20-30% groups are also notably high at 48.9% and 53.1%, respectively, suggesting a trend of increasing dropout rates as the demographic group percentage increases.

Educational implications: The high withdrawal rates indicate a need for enhanced support systems for students in lower demographic groups to improve retention.

Recommendations: high - Implement targeted retention programs for the 0-10% and 10-20% groups. - To address the high withdrawal and failure rates, focused interventions can help improve student engagement and success.

insights.title: High Withdrawal Rate in 0-10% Group
insights.description: The 0-10% group has a withdrawal rate of 53.8%, which is the highest among all groups analyzed, indicating a significant challenge in student retention.

insights.title: Combined Fail and Withdrawal Rates Exceeding Threshold
insights.description: In the 0-10% group, the combined fail (13.8%) and withdrawal (53.8%) rates total 67.6%, significantly exceeding the cohort threshold, indicating a critical need for targeted support.

evidence.context: 0-10% group combined fail and withdrawal rate

insights.title: Consistent Withdrawal Rates Across Groups
insights.description: The withdrawal rates across the 10-20% and 20-30% groups are also notably high at 48.9% and 53.1%, respectively, suggesting a trend of increasing dropout rates as the demographic group percentage increases.

educational_implications: The high withdrawal rates indicate a need for enhanced support systems for students in lower demographic groups to improve retention.

recommendations.action: Implement targeted retention programs for the 0-10% and 10-20% groups.
recommendations.rationale: To address the high withdrawal and failure rates, focused interventions can help improve student engagement and success.

confidence.reason: The data is comprehensive and covers a significant number of students across various demographic groups.

Keep this categorical and descriptive; do not target interventions by demographic group or reverse group rankings.
